chapter_title.en: Chapter 68 – Goodly relationship with the relatives

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/388)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/388)
text: إِنَّ صِلَةَ الرَّحِمِ تُزَكِّي الْأَعْمَالَ وَ تُنْمِي الْأَمْوَالَ وَ تُيَسِّرُ الْحِسَابَ وَ تَدْفَعُ الْبَلْوَى وَ تَزِيدُ فِي الرِّزْقِ .

en.hubeali: Ali, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Husayn Bin Usman, from the one who mentioned it,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The goodly relationships purify the deeds, and develops the wealth, and eases the Reckoning, and repels the afflictions, and increases in the sustenance’.
en.sarwar: 33. Ali has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Husayn ibn ‘Uthman from those whom he has mentioned (in his book) from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Maintaining good relations with relatives cleanses the deeds, increases wealth, eases off the giving of accounts on the Day of Judgment, repels misfortunes and increases one’s means of sustenance.”
